where: Order construction from cart items, shipping, payment, and optional features
why: Orders have 10+ fields: items, address, payment, coupon, gift wrap, insurance, notes, delivery instructions. Most are optional. Price is computed. Cross-field validation needed (gift message requires gift wrap).

where: Reservation construction from room selection, dates, and add-on services
why: Reservations have room type, check-in/out dates, guest count, meal plan, airport pickup, spa packages -- heavily customizable with date-based validation.

where: Custom meal/order construction with item customizations
why: Each food item is configurable: size, toppings, spice level, special instructions, dietary restrictions. Combinations need validation (vegan + cheese = invalid).
Notifications have title, body, optional image, optional action buttons (max 3), priority, scheduling, expiry. SMS can't have images. Expiry must be after schedule time.

where: Booking construction from show, seats, snacks, and optional add-ons
why: Bookings are assembled step by step: movie, showtime, seats, optional snacks, insurance, promo code. Seat count must match ticket count. Total is computed after discounts.

where: Ride request construction with pickup, dropoff, and ride preferences
why: Ride requests have pickup, dropoff, ride type, passenger count, pet-friendly, accessibility needs, scheduled time. Pool rides have max passenger limits. Scheduled rides need future timestamps.

where: Playlist construction from songs, metadata, and sharing preferences
why: Playlists are built incrementally: add songs one by one, set name, description, cover image, collaborative flag, public/private. Songs must exist. Collaborative playlists need at least 2 members.

where: Log entry construction with structured context and metadata
why: Log entries need level, message, timestamp, optional context map, optional stack trace, optional tags, optional correlation ID. Audit logs require user ID. Security logs require IP address.

where: Payment request construction with method-specific fields and validation
why: Payment requests have amount, currency, method, merchant ID, plus conditional fields: card requires token + billing address, UPI requires VPA. Immutability is critical -- no modification after build().

where: Expense creation with variable participants and split logic
why: Expenses have amount, payer, split type, participants (variable count), category, notes, optional receipt. Percentage splits must total 100%. Exact splits must total the expense amount.
